title: Data Analysis
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Data Analysis
text:
Judge Reliability Harness
RAND researchers developed the Judge Reliability Harness, an open-source library that orchestrates standardized, reproducible evaluations of large language model–based judges through systematic perturbation testing and human-in-the-loop validation.
AI Security Guide and Risk Assessment Tool
This guide is a practical, risk-based resource for those navigating the artificial intelligence (AI) security landscape. The guide addresses security of AI systems broadly, and the accompanying tool provides tailored recommendations for security controls.
Provider Perceptions and Experiences Integrating Depression Treatment Into Chronic Healthcare Services in Neno District, Malawi: A Qualitative Study
This study explored healthcare and lay providers’ experiences and perceptions of integrating depression care for patients with chronic conditions at clinics in Malawi.
Evaluating Digital Modernization: Enhancing Analytical and Decision Support in Department of Defense Acquisition Management
The U.S. Department of Defense (DoD) is pursuing digital acquisition to leverage digital technologies and methodologies for enhanced acquisition outcomes. This report fulfills a congressional mandate to assess the state of DoD digital acquisition.
A Scoping Review of How the Candidacy Framework Has Been Used in Research on Access to General Practice
The Candidacy Framework is a useful approach for understanding access to general practice and may help in generating actionable solutions but may be enhanced by further customisation for the specifics of this setting.
Implementation and Use of Technology-Enabled Remote Monitoring for Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease: A Rapid Qualitative Evaluation
To understand what supports good practice in the implementation and use of technology-enabled remote monitoring in the chronic obstructive pulmonary disease care pathway and draw transferable lessons that can inform spread and scale up.
Robust Evaluation of Longitudinal Surrogate Markers with Censored Data
In this paper, we propose robust methods to evaluate a longitudinal surrogate marker in a censored time-to-event outcome setting.
Improving Modeling, Simulation, and Analysis Across the U.S. Space Force System Life Cycle
The authors of this brief summarize research on how the U.S. Space Force can improve its use of modeling and simulation throughout the space system life cycle following interviews, workshops, and a literature review.
More Effective and Efficient Modeling, Simulation, and Analysis Across the U.S. Space Force System Life Cycle
Having conducted and analyzed interviews, participated in workshops, and reviewed literature, the authors of this report examined how the U.S. Space Force can improve its use of modeling and simulation throughout the space system life cycle.
How Will AI Reshape Work?
What do we really know about AI's potential impacts on the workforce? And how might workers, employers, and policymakers prepare for such disruptions?
Using Administrative Claims Data to Address Maternal Health Disparities: Benefits, Pitfalls, and Methodological Solutions
This commentary explores the use of administrative claims data to conduct maternal health disparities research including benefits, pitfalls, and methodological solutions. It recommends data infrastructure improvements and research best practices.
